tactical_shape: Shrewd, well-organised side that never took Canada’s 'decoy' bait
Made key second-half changes through Murat Yakin, with Ruben Vargas and Johan Manzambi starting and both goals coming in a 12-minute burst
Right side/back-post attack used effectively, with Manzambi supplying from the left and Vargas finishing at the back post
Relied on a richly experienced midfield to control a dogfight against Canada
standout_players: Ruben Vargas — scored the opener and rewarded Yakin’s faith
Johan Manzambi — scored the second goal and was a key beneficiary of Switzerland’s sharp start to the second half
Granit Xhaka — anchored a richly experienced Swiss midfield
Ricardo Rodriguez — sparked the game with a driven ball through Canada’s defence
Nico Elvedi — made a heroic block to deny Jonathan David
Gregor Kobel — produced a good first-half save from Ali Ahmed
home_read.momentum: Impressive and deserved winners who topped the group, with momentum strong heading into the knockout stages in Vancouver.

tactical_shape: Jesse Marsch used Alphonso Davies as a 'decoy' rather than playing him
Started with Nathan Saliba and Mathieu Choinière after Stephen Eustáquio was not fit enough to start
Looked to create pressure through hydration-break momentum swings and late substitutions
Pushed for a response after going behind, with Promise David coming off the bench to score
standout_players: Promise David — supersub who scored Canada’s goal one minute after coming on
Maxime Crépeau — made himself big to deny Breel Embolo early
Ali Ahmed — had Canada’s best first-half shot, though it was scuffed
Jonathan David — came close before being denied by Nico Elvedi, then had to settle for a late impact from the other David
fatigue_injuries: Alphonso Davies was not ready to play and was used as a decoy
Ismaël Koné was absent after having his leg shattered six days earlier
Stephen Eustáquio was not fit enough to start
The team is described as injury-hit and facing a shorter four-day turnaround
away_read.momentum: A damaging defeat that sends Canada to Los Angeles on a short turnaround, though Promise David’s goal briefly revived hope and Marsch still frames the trip as a chance to 'electrify the nation'.
